constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and routing infrastructure failures causing 401/404 errors across the entire application
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: GET /api/auth/session → 401 (auth check failing for legitimate users)
GET /api/employees → 404 (employee management completely unavailable)
GET /api/time-entries → 404 (clock-in/out data inaccessible)
GET /api/schedules → 404 (scheduling system unreachable)
GET /api/tasks → 404 (task management broken)
GET /api/payroll/periods → 404 (payroll reports unavailable)
GET /api/activity-log → 404 (audit trail not working)
Multiple dashboard pages return empty/error states due to failed API calls
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible to authenticated users
Schedule management and viewing becomes possible
Task assignment and tracking can function
Payroll report generation becomes available
Activity logging and audit trails work correctly
All dashboard and admin functionality becomes usable
constraintLeaderboard.dismissedReason: False positive — 401s from unauthenticated crawler, not real auth bugs

constraintLeaderboard.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures are blocking ALL app functionality. Clerk auth integration is misconfigured causing 401 errors across the application, and database queries are failing with connection errors.
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: 401 Unauthorized errors on /api/auth/session endpoint
Multiple pages failing to load due to auth failures
Database connection timeouts in server logs
Failed API requests returning 500 errors due to DB connection issues
Users unable to access protected routes
unblocks: Users can successfully log in and access the application
Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible
Schedule viewing and task management becomes operational
Payroll reports can be generated
All API endpoints can serve authenticated requests
Database queries can execute successfully
constraintLeaderboard.dismissedReason: False positive — 401s from unauthenticated crawler, not real auth bugs

constraintLeaderboard.description: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
constraintLeaderboard.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly
evidenceFromCrawl: POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 200 success even when user is 5000+ meters from store location
Geofence validation function exists but result is ignored in the route handler
No error logging when geofence check fails
Database allows time_entries to be created without location verification
Employee can clock in from home, work 8 hours at store, clock out from home — system accepts all events
unblocks: Legal compliance for time tracking
Trust in payroll accuracy
Ability to enforce on-site work requirements
Foundation for future geo-based features (shift reminders, proximity alerts)
Manager confidence in reported work hours
constraintLeaderboard.dismissedReason: False positive — 401s from unauthenticated crawler, not real auth bugs

failedAttempts.constraint: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
failedAttempts.reason: Geofencing validation constraint is NOT resolved. The constraint requires that POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 403 when user is >100m from store, but the codebase provided shows NO geofence validation implementation in the time entries route handler. The crawl verification only checks that pages load (which they do), but does NOT verify the actual geofence enforcement behavior. The re-crawl results show WebSocket connection failures and page load issues, but these are separate from the geofencing constraint. Critical: zero of the 6 planned steps have been completed.

activeConstraint.description: Critical authentication and authorization failures across the entire application preventing any user functionality
activeConstraint.why: Auth is the foundation — without working authentication, ZERO features are accessible. Clock-in, scheduling, payroll, tasks all require auth to function. This is a complete blocker.
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible to employees
Schedule management becomes usable for admins
Payroll reports can be generated
Task assignment can be accessed
All RBAC-protected features become functional
Real user testing of other features becomes possible
evidenceFromCrawl: 401 Unauthorized on GET /api/auth/session (prevents any authenticated requests)
403 Forbidden on POST /api/time-entries/clock-in (blocks primary employee workflow)
401 Unauthorized on GET /api/schedules (blocks schedule viewing)
401 Unauthorized on GET /api/employees (blocks member directory)
401 Unauthorized on POST /api/tasks (blocks task creation)
Network error on /dashboard after login attempt (suggests auth middleware failure)
Multiple routes returning 401/403 consistently across all test runs
activeConstraint.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Audit Clerk middleware integration — verify clerkMiddleware is properly configured, check if requireAuth() is blocking legitimate requests, ensure session tokens are being validated correctly
successCriteria: Middleware logs show successful session validation
Test user can authenticate and receive valid session token
Auth middleware passes requests with valid Clerk tokens

steps.description: Fix session endpoint to properly return Clerk session data — ensure GET /api/auth/session returns user object with role and permissions, not 401
successCriteria: GET /api/auth/session returns 200 with user data when authenticated
Response includes userId, role, and permissions array
Unauthenticated requests return 401 with proper error shape

steps.description: Verify RBAC checks in clock-in endpoint — ensure employees have 'time:clock_in' permission, check if permission string matches Clerk metadata structure
successCriteria: Employee role has time:clock_in permission in Clerk dashboard
POST /api/time-entries/clock-in accepts requests from authenticated employees
Permission check logic matches actual Clerk permission format

steps.description: Fix schedule viewing permissions — ensure both employees (viewing their own schedule) and admins (viewing all schedules) can access GET /api/schedules with proper RBAC filtering
successCriteria: Employees can view their own schedule (filtered by employeeId)
Admins can view all schedules
GET /api/schedules returns 200 for authorized users

steps.description: Fix member directory access — verify permission checks allow appropriate roles to view employee list, ensure owner/admin have access
successCriteria: GET /api/employees returns 200 for owner and admin roles
Response includes employee list with appropriate data filtering by role
Employee role can view basic directory (name, photo, role) but not sensitive data

steps.description: Enable task creation for admins — verify POST /api/tasks has correct permission checks and accepts valid task payloads
successCriteria: Admin role can create tasks via POST /api/tasks
Valid task payload passes Zod validation
Task is saved to database and returns 201 with created task object

steps.description: Verify API client includes Clerk session token in all requests — ensure Authorization header or cookie is properly set on fetch calls
successCriteria: All API requests include valid Clerk session token
Token refresh logic handles expired sessions
Network inspector shows Authorization header on API calls

steps.description: Verify Clerk middleware is applied BEFORE route handlers in Express middleware chain — ensure clerkMiddleware() is called before any protected routes
successCriteria: Middleware order shows clerkMiddleware before route mounting
Test request logs show Clerk auth running first
Auth context is available to all route handlers

activeConstraint.completionCriteria: All protected API endpoints return 200 for authenticated users with appropriate roles. Crawl shows zero 401/403 errors on routes that should be accessible. Test employee can clock in, view schedule, and see tasks. Test admin can view member directory and create tasks.
